key_topics: Real number system: rational vs irrational, approximating irrationals
Integer exponents and scientific notation
Linear equations: solving in one variable, special cases
Systems of linear equations: graphing, substitution, elimination
Linear functions: slope, intercepts, slope-intercept form
Functions: definition, domain, range, notation
Comparing linear and exponential functions
Geometry: transformations — congruence and similarity
Pythagorean theorem: proof and applications
Volume of cones, cylinders, spheres
Statistics: bivariate data, scatter plots, lines of best fit
Introduction to quadratic expressions
competences: Solve systems of equations using three methods
Write and interpret linear equations from graphs and tables
Apply the Pythagorean theorem to 2D and 3D problems
Analyse scatter plots and use lines of best fit for predictions
Understand function notation and evaluate functions
typical_mistakes: Slope calculation errors (using y/x instead of rise/run)
Sign errors solving systems by elimination
Forgetting to check solution in both equations of a system
Pythagorean theorem applied to non-right triangles
Confusing positive and negative correlation in scatter plots

key_topics: Reading: American and world literature — classic and contemporary
Shakespeare: Macbeth, Hamlet, or Julius Caesar (excerpts/full)
19th century American literature: Poe, Hawthorne, Twain
Literary movements: Romanticism, Realism, Naturalism
Rhetoric: analysing speeches and essays (MLK, Lincoln, Frederick Douglass)
Writing: extended literary analysis (5-7 paragraphs)
Argumentative research paper: 5+ pages, 8+ sources
Grammar: syntactic sophistication, varied sentence construction
College-prep vocabulary: SAT/ACT word lists and academic vocabulary
Speaking and listening: formal debate, multimedia presentation
competences: Write sophisticated multi-paragraph literary analysis with close reading
Complete extended argumentative research paper with full MLA citation
Demonstrate mastery of grade 8 grammar and style
typical_mistakes: Retelling plot instead of analysis in literary essays
Weak thesis statements that describe instead of argue
Not integrating and analysing evidence effectively
Sentence structure errors in complex syntax

key_topics: World War I: causes (MAIN — Militarism, Alliances, Imperialism, Nationalism), key events
Aftermath: Treaty of Versailles, League of Nations
Between the Wars: Great Depression, rise of totalitarianism
World War II: causes, key theatres, Holocaust, atomic bombs
Cold War: containment, proxy wars (Korea, Vietnam), détente
Decolonisation: Africa and Asia independence movements
Civil Rights Movement: detailed study
Contemporary World: globalisation, terrorism, climate change
US Government and Civics: detailed study for civic literacy
competences: Explain the complex causes of both World Wars
Analyse the Holocaust and its significance in history
Evaluate the Cold War from multiple perspectives
Demonstrate civic literacy including constitutional principles
typical_mistakes: Reducing WWI to a single assassination event
Confusing Korean and Vietnam War contexts
Not understanding the Separation of Powers in depth

key_topics: Biology: ecology — population dynamics, carrying capacity, succession
Human impact on ecosystems: deforestation, climate change, pollution
Chemistry: stoichiometry — mole concept, molar mass, mole ratios
Solutions: solubility, concentration, acids and bases, pH
Organic chemistry: introduction to carbon compounds
Physics: waves — mechanical and electromagnetic, wave equations
Optics: reflection, refraction, lenses and mirrors
Electromagnetism: electric circuits, Ohm's Law, electromagnetic induction
Nuclear physics: radioactivity, fission, fusion, nuclear energy
competences: Perform stoichiometric calculations using mole concept
Describe wave behaviour with equations (v = fλ)
Apply Ohm's Law to circuit calculations
Analyse human impact on ecosystems with data
typical_mistakes: Stoichiometry ratio errors (using wrong mole ratios from equation)
Confusing fission and fusion in nuclear reactions
